title: There is some secret information here
excerpt:
Hi Senior Monday.com users, 
We are using a dashboard for the whole company to see the project overview status. Since the teammate ppl number is huge, we set up the “Team” group + each relative boards have the subscripti…

posts.author: Han (Kuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Senior Monday.com users,

We are using a dashboard for the whole company to see the project overview status. Since the teammate ppl number is huge, we set up the “Team” group + each relative boards have the subscription of the team group.

However, we are still see the following info “There is some secret information here”, which is quite bizarre. By any change, does anyone can share the possible mistake i made?

MicrosoftTeams-image2496×1604 441 KB

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Han - this is due to the individuals viewing the dashboard not having access to the board or possibly the columns you are reporting on. Users need to have access to the source board in order to have access to the data behind it in a dashboard.

Hope this helps!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: rossg (Ross G)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m running into the same issue, but the person who can’t see it has access to the board and is an admin of the workspace.

I can’t see any reason why they wouldn’t be able to view it.

Does anyone know why this keeps happening / why this message shows?

posts.author: EliW (Eli)
posts.cookedHtml: Did anyone get back to Ross? I have the same problem.

posts.author: Megan345 (Megan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Eli, a few things you could check:

  • Check the viewing permissions on the source board(s) - are some items hidden?
  • Are the widgets displaying data from mirror columns? Do the users have access to those source boards?
